001package guiEmailLogin;
002
003import javafx.geometry.Pos;
004import javafx.scene.Scene;
005import javafx.scene.control.Alert;
006import javafx.scene.control.Button;
007import javafx.scene.control.Label;
008import javafx.scene.control.PasswordField;
009import javafx.scene.control.TextField;
010import javafx.scene.control.Alert.AlertType;
011import javafx.scene.layout.Pane;
012import javafx.scene.text.Font;
013import javafx.stage.Stage;
014
015/*******
016 * <p> Title: ViewEmailLogin Class. </p>
017 * 
018 * <p> Description: The ViewEmailLogin Page allows users to authenticate with their 
019 * email address and password that they created during email registration.</p>
020 * 
021 * <p> Copyright: Modified 2026 </p>
022 * 
023 * @version 1.00                2026-02-09 Initial version
024 *  
025 */
026
027public class ViewEmailLogin {
028        
029        /*-********************************************************************************************
030
031        Attributes
032        
033        */
034        
035        // These are the application values required by the user interface
036        
037        private static double width = applicationMain.FoundationsMain.WINDOW_WIDTH;
038        private static double height = applicationMain.FoundationsMain.WINDOW_HEIGHT;
039        
040        private static Label label_ApplicationTitle = 
041                        new Label("Foundation Application Email Login");
042    protected static Label label_EmailLogin = new Label("Email Account Login");
043    protected static Label label_Instructions = new Label("Enter your email address and password.");
044    
045    protected static Label label_Email = new Label("Email Address:");
046    protected static TextField text_Email = new TextField();
047    
048    protected static Label label_Password = new Label("Password:");
049    protected static PasswordField text_Password = new PasswordField();
050    
051    protected static Button button_Login = new Button("Login");
052
053        // Alerts for error conditions
054    protected static Alert alertLoginError = new Alert(AlertType.ERROR);
055
056    protected static Button button_Cancel = new Button("Cancel");
057
058        // These attributes are used to configure the page and populate it with this user's information
059        private static ViewEmailLogin theView;          // Is instantiation of the class needed?
060
061        protected static Stage theStage;                        // The Stage that JavaFX has established for us
062        private static Pane theRootPane;                        // The Pane that holds all the GUI widgets 
063        public static Scene theEmailLoginScene = null;  
064        
065
066        /*-********************************************************************************************
067
068        Constructors
069        
070        */
071
072        /**********
073         * <p> Method: displayEmailLogin(Stage ps) </p>
074         * 
075         * <p> Description: This method is the single entry point from outside this package to cause
076         * the EmailLogin page to be displayed.</p>
077         * 
078         * @param ps specifies the JavaFX Stage to be used for this GUI and it's methods
079         * 
080         */
081        public static void displayEmailLogin(Stage ps) {
082                
083                // Establish the references to the GUI
084                theStage = ps;
085                
086                if (theView == null) theView = new ViewEmailLogin();
087                
088                text_Email.setText("");                 // Clear the input fields
089                text_Password.setText("");
090                
091        // Place all of the established GUI elements into the pane
092        theRootPane.getChildren().clear();
093        theRootPane.getChildren().addAll(
094                        label_ApplicationTitle,
095                        label_EmailLogin, 
096                        label_Instructions, 
097                        label_Email, text_Email,
098                        label_Password, text_Password, 
099                        button_Login, 
100                        button_Cancel);         
101
102                // Set the title for the window, display the page
103                theStage.setTitle("CSE 360 Foundation Code: Email Login");      
104        theStage.setScene(theEmailLoginScene);
105                theStage.show();
106        }
107        
108        /**********
109         * <p> Constructor: ViewEmailLogin() </p>
110         * 
111         * <p> Description: This constructor is called just once, the first time email login 
112         * needs to be performed.</p>
113         * 
114         */
115        private ViewEmailLogin() {
116
117                // Create the Pane for the list of widgets and the Scene for the window
118                theRootPane = new Pane();
119                theEmailLoginScene = new Scene(theRootPane, width, height);
120                
121                // Populate the window with the title and other widgets
122                setupLabelUI(label_ApplicationTitle, "Arial", 28, width, Pos.CENTER, 0, 10);
123
124                setupLabelUI(label_EmailLogin, "Arial", 24, width, Pos.CENTER, 0, 60);
125                
126                setupLabelUI(label_Instructions, "Arial", 16, width, Pos.CENTER, 0, 100);
127
128                // Email field
129                setupLabelUI(label_Email, "Arial", 18, 150, Pos.BASELINE_RIGHT, 200, 160);
130                setupTextUI(text_Email, "Arial", 18, 300, Pos.BASELINE_LEFT, 360, 155, true);
131                text_Email.setPromptText("Enter your email");
132
133                // Password field
134                setupLabelUI(label_Password, "Arial", 18, 150, Pos.BASELINE_RIGHT, 200, 220);
135                setupTextUI(text_Password, "Arial", 18, 300, Pos.BASELINE_LEFT, 360, 215, true);
136                text_Password.setPromptText("Enter your password");
137
138                // Set up the Login button
139                setupButtonUI(button_Login, "Dialog", 18, 200, Pos.CENTER, 350, 300);
140                button_Login.setStyle("-fx-background-color: #4285F4; -fx-text-fill: white;");
141                button_Login.setOnAction((_) -> {ControllerEmailLogin.doEmailLogin(theStage); });
142
143                // Set up alerts
144                alertLoginError.setTitle("Login Error");
145                alertLoginError.setHeaderText(null);
146
147                // Set up the Cancel button  
148                setupButtonUI(button_Cancel, "Dialog", 18, 200, Pos.CENTER, 350, 360);
149                button_Cancel.setOnAction((_) -> {ControllerEmailLogin.performCancel(theStage); });
150        }
151
152        /*-***********************************************************************************
153
154        Helper methods used to minimize the number of lines of code needed
155        
156        */
157
158        /**********
159         * Private local method to initialize the standard fields for a label
160         */
161        private void setupLabelUI(Label l, String ff, double f, double w, Pos p, double x, double y){
162                l.setFont(Font.font(ff, f));
163                l.setMinWidth(w);
164                l.setAlignment(p);
165                l.setLayoutX(x);
166                l.setLayoutY(y);                
167        }
168        
169        /**********
170         * Private local method to initialize the standard fields for a button
171         */
172        private void setupButtonUI(Button b, String ff, double f, double w, Pos p, double x, double y){
173                b.setFont(Font.font(ff, f));
174                b.setMinWidth(w);
175                b.setAlignment(p);
176                b.setLayoutX(x);
177                b.setLayoutY(y);                
178        }
179
180        /**********
181         * Private local method to initialize the standard fields for a text input field
182         */
183        private void setupTextUI(TextField t, String ff, double f, double w, Pos p, double x, double y, boolean e){
184                t.setFont(Font.font(ff, f));
185                t.setMinWidth(w);
186                t.setMaxWidth(w);
187                t.setAlignment(p);
188                t.setLayoutX(x);
189                t.setLayoutY(y);                
190                t.setEditable(e);
191        }       
192}
